mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which depress corporate earnings and lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and declining inflation signals strong supply-side dynamics with low inflationary pressure, which supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in interaction scenarios are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts combined with weak GDP growth signals a contraction in demand-side economic activity, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth indicates robust economic expansion, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 300 (CPIAUCSL) signals central bank inaction, which erodes real returns and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A monetary tightening cycle (rising FEDFUNDS) combined with strong labor market conditions (PAYEMS growth) signals inflationary pressure, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and retail sales signals strong supply-side capacity utilization and demand, which supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in interaction scenarios are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).
